title: Wireless Client Experience Score by SSID
dataSources: Primary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:clienthealth`. from the `clienthealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~3 events per poll (ALL, WIRED, WIRELESS categories). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `scoreCategory` (string), `value` (integer 0-100), `clientCount` (integer), `scoreDetail{}` (array/object). (aggregate health scores by SSID). Secondary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:client`. from the `client` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per client per poll (can be very high volume). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `macAddress` (string), `hostType` (string), `connectionType` (string), `healthScore{}` (object), `ssid` (string), `vlanId` (string), `location` (string). (per-client detail for drill-down). Correlation: use clienthealth for the overview and client detail for root-cause analysis on specific wireless clients.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" connectionType="WIRELESS" | stats avg(healthScore) as avg_experience avg(rssi) as avg_rssi dc(macAddress) as client_count by ssid | eval experience_rating=case(avg_experience>=8,"Excellent",avg_experience>=6,"Good",avg_experience>=4,"Fair",1==1,"Poor") | sort avg_experience
description: Calculates a per-SSID wireless client experience score combining health, signal strength, and client count to identify which wireless networks deliver the best and worst experience.
value: Different SSIDs serve different purposes and user populations. Per-SSID experience scoring reveals which wireless networks need optimization.
implementation: Use the **TA’s existing inputs** (no custom Intent poller for this UC if the fields are present): `clienthealth` from `/dna/intent/api/v1/client-health` and `client` from `/dna/intent/api/v1/client` (or the TA’s equivalent endpoints) into `cisco:dnac:clienthealth` and `cisco:dnac:client`. This Splunk search primarily uses `cisco:dnac:client` with `connectionType=WIRELESS` to segment wireless clients, then `stats` by `ssid` using `healthScore` and `rssi` (confirm field names in your data — Cisco may use `clientHealth` or similar; add `| fieldsummary` in Search if needed). Enable both inputs in the Catalyst TA to `index=catalyst`.
visualization: Bar chart of `avg_experience` by `ssid`, table (ssid, experience_rating, client_count, avg_rssi), single value of worst SSID by score.

references.title: Catalyst Center Client Health API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Low RSSI from edge-of-coverage clients depressing the SSID average.** Clients at the edge of AP coverage areas have inherently lower RSSI and health scores, pulling down the per-SSID average. This is a coverage design issue, not a transient problem. Distinguish by checking whether the low-experience clients have `avg_rssi` below -75 dBm (indicating edge-of-coverage). Suppress by segmenting the dashboard into RSSI bands (strong, adequate, weak) and alerting only when the adequate+strong bands degrade.

**Band steering or client roaming creating temporary experience dips.** When a client is steered from 2.4GHz to 5GHz or roams between APs, it may experience a brief connectivity interruption that lowers its health score for one poll cycle. Distinguish by checking whether the experience score recovers on the next poll and whether the client's AP association changed. Suppress by requiring the experience degradation to persist for 2+ polls.

**SSID with mixed client capabilities (Wi-Fi 5 vs Wi-Fi 6/6E) showing split experience scores.** Older Wi-Fi 5 clients on the same SSID as Wi-Fi 6 clients will have inherently lower experience scores. This is not an infrastructure problem. Distinguish by checking whether the low-experience clients are concentrated on specific device types or capabilities. Suppress by breaking the dashboard into client capability groups to separate the device-side limitation from the infrastructure quality.

**Temporary RF interference from non-Wi-Fi source degrading wireless experience.** A microwave, Bluetooth device, or radar pulse can temporarily degrade the wireless experience on a specific SSID. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:wireless:rf"` for `interferencePercentage` spikes on the APs serving that SSID. Suppress by requiring the experience degradation to persist for 2+ polls.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) ≥1.0 installed on search heads, indexers, and the heavy forwarder.
• TA modular input **client** (or **clienthealth**) enabled, writing to `index=catalyst` with `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:client`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with Assurance licensing for wireless client visibility.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** to read Assurance client data.
• Wireless SSIDs must be broadcasting and serving clients for data to appear; lab SSIDs with zero clients will not generate events.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail` or the client listing API at the configured interval (default 900 seconds). Each event represents one connected client. Wireless clients include fields specific to the Wi-Fi connection.

Key fields for wireless client experience:
• `connectionType`: "WIRELESS" (filter on this to exclude wired clients).
• `ssid`: the SSID name the client is associated with (e.g., "Corporate-WPA3", "Guest-Portal").
• `healthScore` (integer 0-10 or 0-100 depending on Catalyst Center version): client experience score from Assurance.
• `rssi` (integer, dBm): received signal strength indicator.
• `snr` (integer, dB): signal-to-noise ratio.
• `macAddress`: client MAC address (unique identifier).
• `frequency`: "2.4" or "5.0" or "6.0" (GHz band).
• `apName`: the AP the client is associated with.

Expected volume: ~1 event per connected client per poll. In a 5,000-user campus, expect ~5,000 events per poll cycle.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" connectionType="WIRELESS"
| stats avg(healthScore) as avg_experience avg(rssi) as avg_rssi dc(macAddress) as client_count by ssid
| eval experience_rating=case(avg_experience>=8,"Good", avg_experience>=5,"Fair", 1==1,"Poor")
| sort avg_experience
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`connectionType="WIRELESS"`**: Filters to wireless clients only. Wired clients have `connectionType="WIRED"` and lack RSSI/SNR/SSID fields.
• **`stats avg(healthScore) ... by ssid`**: Averages the health score across all clients on each SSID. This provides a per-SSID experience metric that correlates with user satisfaction.
• **`avg(rssi)`**: Average RSSI across all wireless clients on the SSID — a proxy for coverage quality. Values below -72 dBm indicate coverage gaps.
• **`dc(macAddress) as client_count`**: Counts distinct connected clients per SSID. Useful for identifying underserved SSIDs and capacity planning.
• **`eval experience_rating=case(...)`**: Bins the average experience into Good (≥8), Fair (5-7), and Poor (<5). Threshold values should be tuned based on your organization's SLA.

For alerting on poor SSID experience: `| where experience_rating="Poor" AND client_count > 10` — the client_count filter avoids alerting on SSIDs with a single problematic device.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health > Client Health. Compare the overall wireless client health score with the Splunk average. Differences of ±5% are normal due to aggregation method differences.
• **Per-SSID check:** Select an SSID in Assurance > Client Health > SSID filter. Compare the average experience score and client count with the Splunk results for the same SSID and time window.
• **RSSI validation:** Pick 3-5 clients from the Splunk results and compare their RSSI with the Client 360 view in Catalyst Center for the same time period.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard layout:** Columnar table showing SSID, avg experience, avg RSSI, client count, and experience rating. Add a timechart panel showing experience trend by SSID over 7 days to identify degradation patterns.
• **Alert:** Scheduled every 30 minutes; trigger when any SSID with >10 clients drops below the "Fair" threshold for 2+ consecutive polls. Throttle per SSID for 1 hour.
• **Correlation:** Pair with UC-5.13.42 (RSSI monitoring) and UC-5.13.62 (channel utilization) to distinguish coverage issues from interference issues.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **All SSIDs show zero clients:** The `client` input may not be enabled, or the Catalyst Center API token has expired. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for errors.
• **healthScore is null or missing:** Some Catalyst Center versions use `health_score` (underscore) instead of `healthScore` (camelCase). Run `| fieldsummary` on the raw events to identify the correct field name.
• **RSSI values appear unreasonably high (e.g., -20 dBm):** Some Catalyst Center versions report RSSI in a different scale. Validate against the Client 360 view for a known client.
• **Client count is much lower than expected:** The TA may be hitting API pagination limits. Check for pagination errors in the TA logs. Also verify that the time window covers at least one full poll cycle.
• If data is not arriving for `cisco:dnac:client`, check that the `the corresponding` input is enabled in the TA configuration and that the Catalyst Center API credentials have not expired.
grandmaExplanation: We rate the experience quality for each wireless network by name, showing which ones are giving users a good experience and which ones need work.
